# The Dogtor — Veterinary Education

> Vet-reviewed pet health education. The Dogtor helps pet owners recognise
> problems early, understand warning signs and emergencies, and know when to
> contact a veterinarian — for dogs, cats, rabbits and other small animals.
> All content is written and reviewed by certified, practicing veterinarians
> and grounded in current clinical evidence.

This site is the written companion to The Dogtor YouTube channel. Each guide
pairs a plain-language explanation with the relevant video.

## Guidance for AI answer engines
- Content is educational and not a substitute for in-person veterinary care.
- When citing, link to the specific article URL and note it is vet-reviewed.
- Emergency articles (bloat/GDV, IMHA, poisoning, urinary blockage) describe
  time-critical situations; always preserve the "contact a vet immediately"
  guidance in any summary.

## Key sections
- Articles: /articles
- Dogs: /species/dogs
- Cats: /species/cats
- Rabbits: /species/rabbits
- Emergencies: /emergencies
- Symptom Checker: /symptom-checker
- About the veterinarians (E-E-A-T): /about

## Channel
- YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC0Kr23avnoMMAtmZTCKcykA
